summary: Backend-only verification of R1 HTF trend detector refinement in smc_engine.detect_structure. All 127 pytest tests pass (including 16 new R1 tests + 108 pre-existing). Benchmark harness prints RESULT: PASS. False-NEUTRAL rate on trending scenarios dropped 38.0%->25.0% (104/800 absolute, 34.2% relative). Correct direction rose 62.0%->75.0%. Sideways false-positive flips dropped 27.0%->21.2% (did NOT increase). Code footprint confined to smc_engine.py + two new test files. SWING_WINDOW=5 and EQ_TOLERANCE_BPS=5.0 unchanged.
critical_code_review_comments: R1 change is well-scoped: single helper `_bps_diff` + revised `detect_structure` logic in smc_engine.py. No other production file modified.
Benchmark harness embeds a verbatim copy of the OLD detector for A/B — good regression discipline.
Sideways/choppy scenarios saw slight improvement (fewer false directional flips), so anti-whipsaw is preserved.
context_for_next_testing_agent: R1 refinement to HTF trend detector verified. git diff shows only backend/strategy/smc_engine.py modified plus two new test files (test_htf_structure_r1.py, benchmark_htf_r1.py). iteration_3 telegram fix already committed prior to HEAD. All constraints (SWING_WINDOW, BOS, CHoCH, OB, FVG, sweeps, risk, UI) untouched. Benchmark seed=7,n=200/scenario.
